markets.no_sub_title: Walk On
markets.rules_primary: If the play by play or color commentator(s) says Walk On as part of 2026 Men's College Basketball West Coast conference tournament champion, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
Video of the 2026 Men's College Basketball West Coast conference tournament champion will be primarily used to resolve the market; if a consensus by Kalshi employees cannot be reached using video, internal & external (official) transcripts of the 2026 Men's College Basketball West Coast conference tournament champion will be used.

Payout Criterion: 
- The exact phrase/word, or a plural or possessive form of the phrase/word, must be used. Grammatical/tense inflections are otherwise not included. 
- Commentary will count once tip off has occurred and end once the game has concluded. Should the game go into overtime, the commentary will continue to count until the game has ended. If the final play is in review, commentary will end following the referee decision.
- Promotional content aired during the event will count, but advertisements will not. 
- Except as otherwise specified, previously aired content, archival footage, and recordings captured at times, places, or events wholly separate from the featured address will not count.
- Please see full rules for more details.

Event does not qualify:
- If an event is definitively cancelled or the event fails to qualify under the Payout Criterion, or any other circumstance prevents normal resolution, then the market for "Event does not qualify" resolves to Yes and all other markets will resolve to No.  

Postponement:
- If the event is postponed, and such a postponement is announced by the end of the following calendar day to be rescheduled to a time within 14 calendar days, the markets shall remain open.

markets.yes_sub_title: Walk On

markets.no_sub_title: Wild Pitch
markets.rules_primary: If the play by play or color commentator(s) says Wild Pitch as part of Italy vs USA professional baseball game originally scheduled for Mar 10, 2026 at 9:00 PM EDT, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
Video of the Italy vs USA professional baseball game originally scheduled for Mar 10, 2026 at 9:00 PM EDT will be primarily used to resolve the market; if a consensus by Kalshi employees cannot be reached using video, internal & external (official) transcripts of the Italy vs USA professional baseball game originally scheduled for Mar 10, 2026 at 9:00 PM EDT will be used.

Payout Criterion: 
- The exact phrase/word, or a plural or possessive form of the phrase/word, must be used. Grammatical/tense inflections are otherwise not included. 
- Commentary will count following the first pitch and end once 9th inning has concluded or the mercy rule is enacted. The mercy rule will apply if a game ends from a team leading by at least 15 runs after the fifth inning or by at least 10 runs after the seventh inning. Should the game go into extra innings, the commentary will count. Commentary will no longer count following the conclusion of the final inning, if the final play is in review commentary will end following the umpire decision.
- Promotional content aired during the event will count, but advertisements will not. 
- Except as otherwise specified, previously aired content, archival footage, and recordings captured at times, places, or events wholly separate from the featured address will not count.
- Please see full rules for more details.

Event does not qualify:
- If an event is definitively cancelled or the event fails to qualify under the Payout Criterion, or any other circumstance prevents normal resolution, then the market for "Event does not qualify" resolves to Yes and all other markets will resolve to No.   

Postponement:
- If the event is postponed, and such a postponement is announced by the end of the following calendar day to be rescheduled to a time within 14 calendar days, the markets shall remain open.
